farCry7ReleaseStatus.title: Far Cry 7 Release Date and Latest Development Updates
farCry7ReleaseStatus.intro: Ubisoft has confirmed that two Far Cry projects are in development. A final title, release date, and dedicated reveal for the next mainline game have not been published.

milestones.title: Two Far Cry Projects Enter Development
milestones.description: Reports identified a new mainline Far Cry game and a separate extraction-focused multiplayer project. The mainline project became known as Project Blackbird, while the multiplayer project used the codename Project Maverick.

milestones.title: Development Target Moves to 2026
milestones.description: Project Blackbird and Project Maverick were reported to have moved from an internal 2025 target to 2026 as Ubisoft worked on major changes to the series formula.

milestones.title: More Developers Move to Project Blackbird
milestones.description: Ubisoft transferred developers from the rebooted Project Maverick team to Project Blackbird. Playtesting had begun, with development continuing toward a 2026 launch.

milestones.title: Ubisoft Confirms Two Far Cry Projects
milestones.description: Ubisoft CEO Yves Guillemot stated that the company had two promising Far Cry projects in development under the newly structured Vantage Studios organization.

milestones.title: Release Date Has Not Been Published
milestones.description: Ubisoft has not released a dedicated Far Cry 7 product page, final title, launch date, preorder listing, or official release window.

farCry7TrailerReveal.title: Far Cry 7 Trailer, Gameplay and Screenshot Tracker
farCry7TrailerReveal.intro: This tracker covers the official announcement trailer, gameplay presentation, screenshots, showcase appearances, and developer updates for the next Far Cry game. Videos using fan-made footage, edited Far Cry clips, or concept trailers are not part of Ubisoft's official reveal campaign.

items.title: Official Announcement Trailer
items.description: Ubisoft has not uploaded an announcement trailer carrying the Far Cry 7 name or Project Blackbird codename.

items.title: Official Gameplay Reveal
items.description: No official gameplay demonstration, developer walkthrough, or playable presentation has been published.

items.title: Official Screenshots and Key Art
items.description: Ubisoft has not released screenshots, cover artwork, character renders, or an official Far Cry 7 logo.

items.title: Two Far Cry Projects Confirmed
items.description: Ubisoft CEO Yves Guillemot confirmed that two promising Far Cry projects were in development, without revealing their final names or showing footage.

farCry7Platforms.title: Far Cry 7 Platforms, PC Requirements and Store Availability
farCry7Platforms.intro: Project Blackbird is being developed as the next mainline Far Cry project and has been associated with Ubisoft's Snowdrop engine. Official hardware requirements, console editions, storefronts, performance modes, cross-play features, and subscription availability have not been listed.

platforms.systemRequirements: Minimum and recommended specifications have not been published
platforms.details: CPU, GPU, memory, storage, operating system, DirectX version, ray tracing support, and upscaling options remain unlisted.
No PlayStation Store page, performance mode, resolution target, PS5 Pro enhancement, or DualSense feature list has been published.
No Xbox Store page, performance target, Smart Delivery information, or Xbox Play Anywhere support has been published.
Ubisoft has not listed previous-generation console versions.
No Nintendo edition, cloud version, or Nintendo eShop listing has been published.

platforms.systemRequirements: Subscription details have not been published
platforms.details: Ubisoft has not announced Ubisoft+ access, Xbox Game Pass availability, PlayStation Plus availability, or cloud streaming support.

platforms.systemRequirements: Final technical targets have not been published
platforms.details: Development reporting identifies Snowdrop as the engine replacing the Dunia technology used by recent mainline Far Cry games.

farCry7StoryCharacters.title: Far Cry 7 Story, Characters and Sons of Truth
farCry7StoryCharacters.intro: The reported campaign follows a wealthy family targeted by the Sons of Truth. Its nonlinear rescue structure allows missions and character outcomes to change during the campaign.

characters.name: The Player Character
characters.description: The protagonist is a member of a wealthy family whose relatives have been kidnapped. The central objective is to locate and rescue the remaining family members before the campaign timer expires.

characters.name: The Kidnapped Family
characters.description: Family members can be rescued in different orders during the nonlinear campaign. Their survival affects the story, and rescuing every family member is tied to full campaign completion.

characters.name: Sons of Truth
characters.description: The Sons of Truth is the organization responsible for kidnapping the protagonist's family. The wider campaign objective centers on finding the captives and bringing down the group.

characters.name: Interrogation Targets
characters.description: Enemies can be questioned for information about missing family members. Interrogation outcomes vary because targets may provide information, remain silent, give false directions, or escape.

characters.name: Campaign Timer
characters.description: The rescue campaign has been described as 72 in-game hours, equivalent to approximately 24 real-time hours. Entering a safehouse pauses the timer while the player prepares for the next operation.

characters.name: Main Villain
characters.description: No individual leader or primary villain has been named. The Sons of Truth remains the central antagonist identified in current story details.

characters.name: Cillian Murphy
characters.role: Not Part of the Announced Cast
characters.description: The source behind images that triggered casting speculation stated that the Cillian Murphy image did not mean the actor would appear in Far Cry 7.

farCry7SettingMap.title: Far Cry 7 Map, Setting and Open-World Structure
farCry7SettingMap.intro: Project Blackbird is being designed as a nonlinear open-world campaign built around rescuing members of the Beckett family. Ubisoft has not published the final country, region, or map name, while the Alaskan wilderness described in earlier coverage belongs to the separate Project Maverick multiplayer game.

faqs.title: Project Blackbird's Main Setting
faqs.summary: The main campaign follows a wealthy family kidnapped by a conspiracy cult connected to hallucinogenic experiments.
details: The playable story centers on members of the Beckett family.
The opposing group has been described as a conspiracy cult.
The cult's activities include experiments involving hallucinogens, animals, and children.
Ubisoft has not released an official map name, country, or complete world layout.

faqs.title: Nonlinear Rescue Map
faqs.summary: Family members can be pursued in different orders, turning the open world into a set of connected rescue routes rather than one fixed mission path.
details: Rescue targets do not need to be completed in a predetermined order.
Different routes can lead players toward different family members.
The survival of each family member affects campaign outcomes.
Rescuing every family member safely is tied to full campaign completion.

faqs.title: Safehouses
faqs.summary: Safehouses are important strategic locations because entering one pauses the campaign timer.
details: The 72-hour in-game countdown pauses inside safehouses.
Safehouses provide protected breaks between rescue objectives.
Players can use these locations to manage time before returning to the open world.

faqs.title: Traversal and Movement
faqs.summary: The movement system has been expanded to support faster and more tactical exploration.
details: Tactical sprinting is part of the updated movement design.
Sliding supports faster movement through combat areas.
Vaulting expands how players cross obstacles and structures.
The movement overhaul is shared with technology developed during the two projects' earlier common production.

faqs.title: Looting and Inventory
faqs.summary: Project Blackbird incorporates a stronger focus on collecting and managing items while exploring the world.
details: Looting has a larger role in the campaign's survival-focused structure.
The inventory system supports the player's race to rescue the kidnapped family.
Several inventory concepts originated from development shared with Project Maverick.

faqs.title: Project Maverick's Alaskan Wilderness
faqs.summary: The snowy Alaskan environment is associated with the standalone multiplayer project rather than Project Blackbird's main campaign.
details: Maverick's world is based on a fictional Alaskan wilderness.
The environment includes severe weather and dangerous wildlife.
Players face AI-controlled soldiers and other human players.
Earlier designs included resource gathering, hideouts, survival crafting, and extraction zones.

farCry7GameplayTimer.title: Far Cry 7 Gameplay Timer, Rescues and Interrogations
farCry7GameplayTimer.intro: The campaign is structured around rescuing the Beckett family before time expires. Players can choose which rescue targets to pursue, pause the clock at safehouses, and interrogate enemies to locate missing family members.

mechanics.name: Campaign Time Limit
mechanics.description: The complete rescue campaign takes place across a 72-hour in-game countdown.
mechanics.playerImpact: Players must balance exploration, combat, interrogations, and rescue objectives against the remaining time.

mechanics.name: Real-Time Length
mechanics.description: The 72-hour game clock corresponds to approximately 24 hours of real gameplay time.
mechanics.playerImpact: Time continues moving while the player operates outside protected safehouses.

mechanics.name: Wristwatch Display
mechanics.description: The remaining time is represented by a watch worn by the player character.
mechanics.playerImpact: Players can track the campaign clock directly while moving through the world.

mechanics.name: Safehouse Pause
mechanics.description: Entering a safehouse stops the countdown until the player returns to the active world.
mechanics.playerImpact: Safehouses provide opportunities to plan the next rescue route without losing campaign time.

mechanics.name: Nonlinear Objectives
mechanics.description: Kidnapped family members do not have to be rescued in a fixed sequence.
mechanics.playerImpact: Players can prioritize targets according to location, available information, and remaining time.

mechanics.name: Family Outcomes
mechanics.description: Family members can survive or perish depending on the player's progress and choices.
mechanics.playerImpact: Failed rescues alter the campaign outcome and prevent a complete family rescue.

mechanics.name: Interrogation Responses
mechanics.description: Interrogated enemies can provide useful information, give false answers, refuse to cooperate, or escape.
mechanics.playerImpact: Players must decide whether information is useful before spending limited time following a lead.

mechanics.name: Full Completion Goal
mechanics.description: Complete campaign progress requires every kidnapped family member to be recovered safely.
mechanics.playerImpact: Efficient routing and successful interrogations are central to reaching the complete ending.

farCry7MultiplayerMaverick.title: Far Cry 7 Multiplayer, Co-op and Project Maverick
farCry7MultiplayerMaverick.intro: The two Far Cry projects originated from a shared production called Talisker before becoming separate games. Blackbird focuses on the timed family-rescue campaign, while Maverick has been developed around extraction, survival, player competition, and team-based play.

comparisons.blackbird: The next mainline Far Cry game and the project commonly referred to as Far Cry 7.
comparisons.maverick: A separate multiplayer-focused Far Cry game.

comparisons.blackbird: A nonlinear open-world campaign about rescuing the kidnapped Beckett family.
comparisons.maverick: An extraction-based survival shooter built around entering a dangerous region, collecting resources, and escaping safely.

comparisons.blackbird: A final map location has not been publicly named.
comparisons.maverick: A fictional Alaskan wilderness featuring severe weather, wild animals, AI soldiers, and rival players.

comparisons.blackbird: The current gameplay outline centers on the mainline rescue campaign.
comparisons.maverick: Earlier designs supported solo play and team-based sessions with teammates sharing a Hideout and stored loot.

comparisons.blackbird: Rescue family members before the 72-hour in-game timer expires.
comparisons.maverick: Gather equipment, complete contracts, survive encounters, and extract with collected resources.

comparisons.blackbird: Family losses and failed rescues affect the campaign story.
comparisons.maverick: The earlier extraction design removed carried gear, perks, and levels after death while preserving items stored in the Hideout.

comparisons.blackbird: Uses tactical sprinting, sliding, vaulting, expanded looting, and inventory management.
comparisons.maverick: Uses related movement, looting, survival, inventory, and resource-gathering systems.

comparisons.blackbird: Playtesting began while additional staff were transferred to keep the mainline project moving forward.
comparisons.maverick: Returned to prototyping after an internal reboot and continued as one of Ubisoft's two Far Cry projects.

comparisons.blackbird: Part of a broader Far Cry pipeline managed within Ubisoft's major-franchise development structure.
comparisons.maverick: Supports Ubisoft's stated goal of making multiplayer a more prominent part of Far Cry's long-term future.

farCry7EngineDevelopment.title: Far Cry 7 Development, Snowdrop Engine and Latest Updates
farCry7EngineDevelopment.intro: Project Blackbird is led by Ubisoft Montreal with support from several Ubisoft studios. Development has included a move away from the Dunia engine, redesigned movement and inventory systems, internal schedule changes, and resources transferred from Project Maverick.

updates.title: Blackbird and Maverick Emerge as Separate Projects
updates.description: The next mainline Far Cry game became known as Project Blackbird, while the standalone multiplayer project became Project Maverick. Both grew from an earlier shared production called Talisker.

updates.title: Snowdrop Engine and Campaign Systems Detailed
updates.description: Development information connected Blackbird to Ubisoft Montreal and the Snowdrop engine. The same update outlined the 72-hour in-game timer, safehouse pauses, nonlinear rescues, and interrogation system.

updates.title: Movement and Inventory Overhaul
updates.description: Blackbird and Maverick were described as moving away from the traditional Far Cry formula with tactical sprinting, sliding, vaulting, updated looting, and expanded inventory systems.

updates.title: Internal Schedule Moves Beyond 2025
updates.description: Development targets for both projects shifted from 2025 into 2026 as Ubisoft addressed production complexity.

updates.title: Maverick Reboot Sends Resources to Blackbird
updates.description: Project Maverick returned to prototyping, while developers from its technical team and Ubisoft Berlin were reassigned to Blackbird.

updates.title: Blackbird Playtesting Underway
updates.description: Project Blackbird entered playtesting with its redesigned gameplay and movement systems. Supporting teams included Ubisoft Montreal, Ubisoft Berlin, Ubisoft Annecy, Red Storm, and the Talisker technical group.

updates.title: Ubisoft Expands Far Cry's Multiplayer Direction
updates.description: Ubisoft CEO Yves Guillemot stated that multiplayer would become a more prominent part of Far Cry so players could remain engaged for longer periods.

updates.title: Beckett Family and Timer Remain in Development Materials
updates.description: Development materials linked to the project continued to reference the Beckett family and the 24-real-hour rescue timer.

updates.title: Ubisoft Confirms Two Far Cry Projects
updates.description: Yves Guillemot confirmed that Ubisoft had two Far Cry projects in development. Project Blackbird remained the mainline title, while Maverick continued as the multiplayer-focused project.

updates.title: Current Public Development Status
updates.description: Ubisoft continues to list two Far Cry projects in development, while the final titles, launch dates, platforms, trailers, and complete gameplay specifications remain unpublished.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Far Cry 7

questions.question: What is Far Cry 7?
questions.answer: Far Cry 7 is the unofficial name for the next mainline Far Cry game, currently in development at Ubisoft under the codename Project Blackbird. Ubisoft has not yet published its final title, release date, or official trailer.

questions.question: When is the Far Cry 7 release date?
questions.answer: Ubisoft has not announced an official Far Cry 7 release date. Development reporting moved the expected window from 2025 into 2026, and the final launch date remains unpublished.

questions.question: What platforms will Far Cry 7 be on?
questions.answer: Far Cry 7 platforms have not been confirmed by Ubisoft. The game is expected to target current-generation consoles and PC, but no official store pages or hardware specifications have been released.

questions.question: What engine does Far Cry 7 use?
questions.answer: Development reporting indicates Far Cry 7 (Project Blackbird) runs on Ubisoft's Snowdrop engine, moving away from the Dunia engine used by earlier mainline Far Cry games.

questions.question: Is there an official Far Cry 7 trailer?
questions.answer: No. Ubisoft has not released an official Far Cry 7 announcement trailer or gameplay reveal. Any trailers currently circulating are fan-made concept videos, not official Ubisoft content.

questions.question: What is Project Maverick in Far Cry 7?
questions.answer: Project Maverick is a separate multiplayer-focused Far Cry game developed alongside Project Blackbird. It is not Far Cry 7, but part of Ubisoft's two-project Far Cry pipeline alongside the mainline campaign.
